info.name: Get a List of All Projects.

items.docs: This endpoint returns an list of all the projects in the Unleash instance.

**Enterprise feature**

Create a new [Unleash project](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/projects).

**Enterprise feature**

Validate a project ID against Unleash's rules

**Enterprise feature**

Update a project with new configuration. Any fields not provided are ignored.

**Enterprise feature**

Permanently delete the provided project. All feature flags in the project must be archived before you can delete it. This permanently deletes the project and its archived flags. It can not be undone.

**Enterprise feature**

Update project enterprise settings with new values. Any fields not provided are ignored.

info.name: Get Users and Groups in Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Get all groups, users and their roles, and available roles for the given project.

**Enterprise feature**

Configure project access for groups and single users. The provided users and groups will be given the roles specified in the payload.

info.name: Set Users and Groups to Roles in the Current Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Sets all groups, users and their roles for the given project, overriding any existing configuration.

**Enterprise feature**

For the provided role, retrieves a list of projects that use this role. For each project it also contains information about how the role used in that project, such as how many users, groups, or service accounts that use the role.

**Enterprise feature**

Sets the roles a user has in the project.

info.name: Remove Project Access for a User

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Removes project access for a user by removing all of its roles for the project.

**Enterprise feature**

Sets the roles a group has in the project.

info.name: Remove Project Access for a Group

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Removes project access for a group by removing all of its roles for the project.

info.name: Get an Overview of a Project.

items.docs: This endpoint returns an overview of the specified projects stats, project health, number of members, which environments are configured, and the features types in the project.

info.name: Get an Overview Project Dora Metrics.

items.docs: This endpoint returns an overview of the specified dora metrics

info.name: Get a List of All Applications for a Project.

params.name: query
params.description: The search query for the application name, sdk, environment

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of applications to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of applications to return in a page. By default it is set to 50.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the results by. By default it is set to "appName".

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order for the sortBy. By default it is det to "asc".

items.docs: This endpoint returns an list of all the applications for a project.

info.name: Get a List of All Flag Creators for a Project.

items.docs: This endpoint returns every user who created a flag in the project.
Returns a list of the outdated SDKS with the applications using them.

info.name: Add an Environment to a Project.

items.docs: This endpoint adds the provided environment to the specified project, with optional support for enabling and disabling change requests for the environment and project.

info.name: Remove an Environment From a Project.

items.docs: This endpoint removes the specified environment from the project.
Sets a default strategy for this environment. Unleash will use this strategy by default when enabling a feature flag. Use the wild card "*" for `:environment` to add to all environments.

info.name: Get a Health Report for a Project.

items.docs: This endpoint returns a health report for the specified project. This data is used for [the technical debt insights](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/technical-debt)
Returns the project-specific [API tokens](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens) that have been created for this project.
Endpoint that allows creation of [project API tokens](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#api-token-visibility) for the specified project.
This operation deletes the API token specified in the request URL. If the token doesn't exist, returns an OK response (status code 200).

info.name: Get an Overview of a Project Insights.

items.docs: This endpoint returns insights into the specified projects stats, health, lead time for changes, feature types used, members and change requests.

**Enterprise feature**

Archive the provided project. All feature flags in the project must be archived before you can archive the project.

**Enterprise feature**

Revive the specified project from the archive.

info.name: Get Aggregated Metered Connections for a Given Time Period.

params.name: grouping
params.description: Whether to aggregate the data by month or by day

params.name: from
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: to
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets metered connections count for the selected period, either aggregated by day or by month.

info.name: Get Aggregated Metered Requests for a Given Time Period.

params.name: grouping
params.description: Whether to aggregate the data by month or by day

params.name: from
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: to
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets metered requests count for the selected period, either aggregated by day or by month.

params.name: from
params.description: The beginning of the date range in yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: to
params.description: The end of the date range in yyyy-MM-dd format

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets high level information about the usage of this Unleash instance, including user, project, and flag information.

**Enterprise feature**

Subscribe to email subscription e.g. monthly productivity report. By default everyone is subscribed. This API is mostly used by hosted Unleash.

**Enterprise feature**

Unsubscribe from email subscription e.g. monthly productivity report. This API is mostly used by hosted Unleash.

**Enterprise feature**

Fetches an array of licensed users with date and count.

**Enterprise feature**

Reports that an Enterprise Edge instance is alive and in use. This is related to billing and usage tracking.

info.name: Get the Number of Change Requests You Can Do Something With

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Returns the number of change requests in this project that your user can move into the next stage by approving or applying them.

info.name: Get All Feedback Events. Limited to 2000 Items.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Get all feedback events.

**Enterprise feature**

Allows users to submit feedback.

**Enterprise feature**

Retrieves all CDN tokens for the specified Edge instance.

**Enterprise feature**

Creates a new CDN token for the specified Edge instance.

**Enterprise feature**

Returns the current settings for SCIM provisioning.

**Enterprise feature**

Sets new settings for SCIM provisioning.

info.name: Generates a New SCIM API Token.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Generates a new SCIM API token. Previously generated tokens will be invalidated.

**Enterprise feature**

Connects to the client features SSE streaming API.

**Enterprise feature**

Initially returns the full set of feature flags available to the provided API key. When called again with the returned etag, only returns the flags that have changed

**Enterprise feature**

Disconnects all streaming clients.

**Enterprise feature**

Fetches analytics data from Plausible API for favorite events over the last 7 days.

info.name: Get Impact Metrics Configurations for a Single Feature

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets all impact metrics configurations associated with a specific feature flag.

info.name: Save Flag Level Impact Metrics Configuration

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Saves a configuration for an impact metric chart associated with a feature flag.

info.name: Get Impact Metrics Configuration for the Instance

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets all impact metrics configurations now associated with any feature flag.

info.name: Save Instance Level Impact Metrics Configuration

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Saves a configuration for an instance level impact metric chart without a feature flag

info.name: Delete Flag Level Impact Metric Configuration

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Deletes an impact metric configuration by its ID.

info.name: Delete Instance Level Impact Metric Configuration

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Deletes an impact metric configuration by its ID.

info.name: Create or Update a Milestone Progression

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Creates or updates a milestone progression by source milestone ID. If a progression with the given source milestone exists, it will be updated; otherwise, a new one will be created.

**Enterprise feature**

This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Deletes an existing milestone progression by source milestone ID.

**Enterprise feature**

This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Resumes all paused milestone progressions for a release plan

**Enterprise feature**

This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Creates or updates a safeguard for a specific feature, environment, and release plan.

**Enterprise feature**

This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Deletes a safeguard by its ID for a specific feature, environment, and release plan.

info.name: Returns the List of Permissions for the User

params.name: id
params.description: a user id

items.docs: Gets a list of permissions for a user, additional project and environment can be specified.
This endpoint returns information on the status the project, including activities, health, resources, and aggregated flag lifecycle data.
Information about the lifecycle stages of the feature.
This will set the feature as completed.
This will set the feature as uncompleted.
Create a new link for a feature.
Update an existing feature link.
Delete a feature link by id.

info.name: Get All Features Lifecycle Stage Count

items.docs: Information about the number of features in each lifecycle stage.
Return all projects and flags that are relevant to the user.
Return personal dashboard project events, owners, user roles and onboarding status

**Enterprise feature**

Moves the specified feature to the new project in the request schema. Requires you to have permissions to move the feature flag in both projects. Features that are included in any active change requests can not be moved.
Validates a feature flag name: checks whether the name is URL-friendly and whether a feature with the given name already exists. Returns 200 if the feature name is compliant and unused.

info.name: Get All Tags for a Feature.

items.docs: Retrieves all the tags for a feature name. If the feature does not exist it returns an empty list.

info.name: Adds a Tag to a Feature.

items.docs: Adds a tag to a feature if the feature and tag type exist in the system. The operation is idempotent, so adding an existing tag will result in a successful response.

info.name: Updates Multiple Tags for a Feature.

items.docs: Receives a list of tags to add and a list of tags to remove that are mandatory but can be empty. All tags under addedTags are first added to the feature and then all tags under removedTags are removed from the feature.

info.name: Removes a Tag From a Feature.

items.docs: Removes a tag from a feature. If the feature exists but the tag does not, it returns a successful response.
Information about the enablement status and strategies for a feature flag in specified environment.
Disable a feature flag in the specified environment.
Enable a feature flag in the specified environment.

info.name: Bulk Enable a List of Features

items.docs: This endpoint enables multiple feature flags.

info.name: Bulk Disable a List of Features

items.docs: This endpoint disables multiple feature flags.
Get strategies defined for a feature flag in the specified environment.

info.name: Add a Strategy to a Feature Flag

items.docs: Add a strategy to a feature flag in the specified environment.
Get a strategy configuration for an environment in a feature flag.
Replace strategy configuration for a feature flag in the specified environment.

info.name: Change Specific Properties of a Strategy

items.docs: Change specific properties of a strategy configuration in a feature flag.

info.name: Delete a Strategy From a Feature Flag

items.docs: Delete a strategy configuration from a feature flag in the specified environment.
Set the sort order of the provided list of strategies.

info.name: Get All Features in a Project

items.docs: A list of all features for the specified project.
Create a new feature flag in a specified project.
Creates a copy of the specified feature flag. The copy can be created in any project.
This endpoint returns the information about the requested feature if the feature belongs to the specified project.
Updates the specified feature if the feature belongs to the specified project. Only the provided properties are updated; any feature properties left out of the request body are left untouched.
Change specific properties of a feature flag.
This endpoint archives the specified feature if the feature belongs to the specified project.

info.name: Mark Features as Stale / Not Stale

items.docs: This endpoint marks the provided list of features as either [stale](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/technical-debt#stale-and-potentially-stale-flags) or not stale depending on the request body you send. Any provided features that don't exist are ignored.

info.name: Get Variants for a Feature in an Environment

items.docs: Returns the variants for a feature in a specific environment. If the feature has no variants it will return an empty array of variants

info.name: Create (overwrite) Variants for a Feature in an Environment

items.docs:
This overwrites the current variants for the feature flag in the :featureName parameter for the :environment parameter.

The backend will validate the input for the following invariants:

* If there are variants, there needs to be at least one variant with `weightType: variable`

* The sum of the weights of variants with `weightType: fix` must be strictly less than 1000 (< 1000)

The backend will also distribute remaining weight up to 1000 after adding the variants with `weightType: fix` togethe

info.name: Patch a Feature's Variants in an Environment

items.docs: Apply a list of patches to the features environments in the specified environment. The patch objects should conform to the [JSON-patch format (RFC 6902)](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6902).

info.name: Create (overwrite) Variants for a Feature Flag in Multiple Environments

items.docs: This overwrites the current variants for the feature flag in the :featureName parameter for the :environment parameter.
This endpoint return info about the archive features impact.

This endpoint archives the specified features. Any features that are already archived or that don't exist are ignored. All existing features (whether already archived or not) that are provided must belong to the specified project.

Validates a constraint definition. Checks whether the context field exists and whether the applied configuration is valid. Additional properties are not allowed on data objects that you send to this endpoint.
This endpoint marks the feature in the url as favorite
This endpoint removes the feature in the url from favorites
This endpoint marks the project in the url as favorite
This endpoint removes the project in the url from favorites

**Enterprise feature**

Returns a list of available permissions
Logs in the user and creates an active session

**Enterprise feature**

Returns the current settings for SAML authentication

**Enterprise feature**

Updates the settings for SAML Authentication

**Enterprise feature**

Returns the current settings for OIDC Authentication

**Enterprise feature**

Configure OpenID Connect as a login provider for Unleash.

**Enterprise feature**

Is simple authentication (username/password) enabled for this server

**Enterprise feature**

Enable or disable simple authentication (username/password)

info.name: Returns the List of Permissions for the Service Account.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets a list of permissions for a service account. Project and environment can be specified.

**Enterprise feature**

Gets an overview of what access all users have to all projects and groups
If the token is valid returns the user that owns the token
Allows users with a valid reset token to reset their password without remembering their old password
Verifies that the password adheres to the [Unleash password guidelines](https://docs.getunleash.io/using-unleash/deploy/configuring-unleash#securing-unleash)
Requests a password reset email for the user. This email can be used to reset the password for a user that has forgotten their password

**Enterprise feature**

Gets usage data per app/endpoint from a prometheus compatible metrics endpoint

info.name: Get Aggregated Traffic Data for a Given Time Period.

params.name: grouping
params.description: Whether to aggregate the data by month or by day

params.name: from
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: to
params.description: The starting date of the traffic data usage search in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets traffic usage data for the selected period, either aggregated by day or by month.

Returns data about the specified application (`appName`). The data contains information on the name of the application, sdkVersion (which sdk reported these metrics, typically `unleash-client-node:3.4.1` or `unleash-client-java:7.1.0`), as well as data about how to display this application in a list.

info.name: Create an Application to Connect Reported Metrics

items.docs: Is used to report usage as well which sdk the application uses
Delete the application specified in the request URL. Returns 200 OK if the application was successfully deleted or if it didn't exist

params.name: query
params.description: The search query for the application name

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of applications to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of applications to return in a page. By default it is set to 1000.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the results by. By default it is set to "appName".

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order for the sortBy. By default it is det to "asc".

items.docs: Returns all applications registered with Unleash. Applications can be created via metrics reporting or manual creation
Returns an overview of the specified application (`appName`).
Returns an overview of the instances for the given `appName` and `environment` that have received traffic in the last 24 hours.
Returns a list of the outdated SDKS with the applications using them.
Get usage metrics for a specific feature for the last 48 hours, grouped by hour

info.name: Last Hour of Usage and a List of Applications That Have Reported Seeing This Feature Flag

items.docs: Separate counts for yes (enabled), no (disabled), as well as how many times each variant was selected during the last hour
Retrieves the stored custom metrics data.
Exposes all custom metrics in Prometheus text format for scraping.

**Enterprise feature**

A user may get relevant notifications from the projects they are a member of

**Enterprise feature**

Allow to select which notifications were read and saving a read date

params.name: projects
params.description: Comma-separated list of project IDs to filter lifecycle trends

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Gets lifecycle trends information showing feature flag progression across development, production, and cleanup stages.

Sends feedback gathered from the Unleash UI to the Unleash server. Must be called with a token with an identifiable user (either from being sent from the UI or from using a [PAT](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#personal-access-tokens)).
Retrieves the full configuration used to set up the Unleash Admin UI.
Sets Cross-Origin Resource Sharing headers for Frontend SDK API.

Updates the feedback with the provided ID. Only provided fields are updated. Fields left out are left untouched. Must be called with a token with an identifiable user (either from being sent from the UI or from using a [PAT](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#personal-access-tokens)).
This operation updates splash settings for a user, indicating that they have seen a particualar splash screen.

info.name: Accepts Errors From the UI Client

items.docs: This endpoint accepts error reports from the UI client, so that we can add observability on UI errors.

**Enterprise feature**

Registers Edge observability metrics from downstream Edge instances to aggregate a comprehensive status view of connected Edges.
This operation accepts batched metrics from any client. Metrics will be inserted into Unleash's metrics storage

This operation accepts a list of tokens to validate. Unleash will validate each token you provide. For each valid token you provide, Unleash will return the token along with its type and which projects it has access to.

**Enterprise feature**

Get a list of user groups for Role-Based Access Control

**Enterprise feature**

Create a new user group for Role-Based Access Control

**Enterprise feature**

Get a single user group by group id

**Enterprise feature**

Update existing user group by group id. It overrides previous group details.

**Enterprise feature**

Delete a single user group by group id

**Enterprise feature**

Deletes all groups managed by SCIM

**Enterprise feature**

Get a list of project, root and custom roles for Role-Based Access Control

**Enterprise feature**

Create a new custom role for Role-Based Access Control

**Enterprise feature**

Get a single role by role id

**Enterprise feature**

Update a custom role by role id

**Enterprise feature**

Delete a custom role by id. You cannot delete built-in roles or roles that are in use.

**Enterprise feature**

Check if the role matches schema and has a unique name
Detailed information about the current user, user permissions and user feedback
Detailed information about the current user root role and project membership
Requires specifying old password and confirming new password

info.name: Get Roles for Currently Logged in User

params.name: projectId
params.description: The id of the project you want to check permissions for

items.docs: Gets roles assigned to currently logged in user. Both explicitly and transitively through group memberships
Gets all inactive users. An inactive user is a user that has not logged in in the last 180 days
Deletes all inactive users. An inactive user is a user that has not logged in in the last 180 days
Validate the password strength. Minimum 10 characters, uppercase letter, number, special character.

params.name: id
params.description: a user id

items.docs: Change password for a user as an admin
Reset user password as an admin
Will return all users and all available root roles for the Unleash instance.
Creates a new user with the given root role.

params.name: q
params.description: The pattern to search in the username or email

items.docs: It will preform a simple search based on name and email matching the given query. Requires minimum 2 characters

info.name: Get Basic User and Group Information

items.docs: Get a subset of user and group information eligible even for non-admin users

items.docs: Get a total count of admins with password, without password and admin service accounts

params.name: id
params.description: a user id

items.docs: Will return a single user by id

params.name: id
params.description: a user id

items.docs: Only the explicitly specified fields get updated.

params.name: id
params.description: a user id

items.docs: Deletes the user with the given userId
Deletes all users managed by SCIM
Retrieves all environments that exist in this Unleash instance.

**Enterprise feature**

Uses the details provided in the payload to create a new environment

info.name: Validates If an Environment Name Exists

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Uses the name provided in the body of the request to validate if the given name exists or not

**Enterprise feature**

Given an environment by name updates the environment with the given payload. Note that `name`, `enabled` and `protected` cannot be changed by this API
Retrieves the environment with `name` if it exists in this Unleash instance

**Enterprise feature**

Given an existing environment by name, this endpoint will attempt to delete it

**Enterprise feature**

Given an existing environment name and a set of options, this will create a copy of that environment

info.name: Get the Environments Available to a Project

items.docs: Gets the environments that are available for this project. An environment is available for a project if enabled in the [project configuration](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/environments#enable-an-environment)
Updates sort orders for the named environments. Environments not specified are unaffected.
Makes it possible to enable this environment for a project. An environment must first be globally enabled using this endpoint before it can be enabled for a project
Removes this environment from the list of available environments for projects to use

info.name: Retrieves Change Request Configuration for a Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Given a projectId, this endpoint will retrieve change request configuration for the project

info.name: Updates Change Request Configuration for an Environment in the Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will change the change request configuration for a given environment, set it to either on/off and optionally configure the number of approvals needed. The minimum number of approvals is 1 and the maximum number is 10. If you provide a number higher than 10 or lower than 1, Unleash will clamp it to the allowed range.

info.name: Get Scheduled Change Requests Matching a Query.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint retrieves basic information about all scheduled change requests that match the search criteria provided in the query parameters. The endpoint will return any change request that matches one or more of the provided parameters. If you use no query parameters, you'll get an empty list.

For instance, to find all the scheduled change requests that either touch feature `MyFeature` or strategy `0D198067-7D55-460C-9EC7-DB86E3AE261A`, you would use the query string

info.name: Create/add Change to a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Given a change request exists, this endpoint will attempt to add a change to

an existing change request for the user. If a change request does not exist,

it will attempt to create one.

info.name: Retrieves Number of Project Change Requests in Each State

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve the number of project change requests that are: approved, applied, rejected, scheduled or awaiting review

info.name: Retrieves Pending Change Requests in Configured Environments

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve the pending change requests in the configured environments for the project, for the current user performing the request.

info.name: Retrieves Pending Change Requests in Configured Environments

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve the pending change requests in the configured environments for the project, for the current user performing the request.

info.name: Retrieves All Change Requests for a Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve all change requests regardless of status for a given project. There's an upper limit of last 300 change requests ordered by creation date.

info.name: Retrieves All Pending Change Requests Referencing a Feature in the Project

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve all pending change requests (change requests with a status of Draft | In review | Approved) referencing the given feature flag name.

info.name: Retrieves One Change Request by ID

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve one change request if it matches the provided id.

info.name: Deletes a Change Request by ID

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will delete one change request if it matches the provided id.

info.name: Edits a Single Change in a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will edit one change from a change request if it matches the provided id. The edit removes previous change and inserts a new one. You

should not rely on the changeId for subsequent edits and always check the most recent changeId.

info.name: Discards a Change From a Change Request by Change ID

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will discard one change from a change request if it matches the provided id.

info.name: This Endpoint Will Update the State of a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will update the state of a change request if the business rules allow it. The state can be one of the following: Draft, In review, Approved, Cancelled, Applied. In order to be approved, the change request must have at least one change and the number of approvals must be greater than or equal to the number of approvals required for the environment.

Once a change request has been approved, it can be applied. Once a change request has been applied, it cannot b

info.name: This Endpoint Will Update the Custom Title of a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests get a default title e.g. Change Request #1. This endpoint allows to make the title

more meaningful and describe the intent behind the Change Request

info.name: This Endpoint Fetches the Requested Approvers of a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests can have requests for review from users. This endpoint fetches the list of requested reviewers for a change request.

info.name: This Endpoint Will Update the Reviewers of a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests can have requests for review from users. This endpoint allows to add or remove reviewers from a change request. The maximum number of reviewers is 10.

info.name: This Endpoint Will Add a Comment to a Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will add a comment to a change request for the user making the request.

info.name: This Endpoint Will Return Users Available to Review/approve This Change Request

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will list users available to approve a change request in this project.

params.name: createdBy
params.description: Filter by change request creator user ID

params.name: requestedApproverId
params.description: Filter by requested approver user ID

params.name: state
params.description: Filter by open / closed change requests. Change requests that are in 'draft', 'in review', 'approved', or 'scheduled' states are considered open. Change requests that are in 'cancelled', 'applied', or 'rejected' states are considered closed.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of change requests to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of change requests to return in a page. By default it is set to 50. The maximum is 1000.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Search and filter change requests by creator and approver.

info.name: Evaluate an Unleash Context Against a Change Request Preview.

items.docs:
**Enterprise feature**

Use the provided `context` to evaluate toggles on this Unleash instance with a change request applied in preview mode. You can use comma-separated values to provide multiple values to each context field. Returns a combinatorial list of all toggles that match the parameters and what they evaluate to.

info.name: Evaluate an Unleash Context Against a Set of Environments and Projects.

items.docs: Deprecated. Will be removed in the next Unleash major update. Use the provided `context`, `environment`, and `projects` to evaluate toggles on this Unleash instance. Returns a list of all toggles that match the parameters and what they evaluate to. The response also contains the input parameters that were provided.

info.name: Batch Evaluate an Unleash Context Against a Set of Environments and Projects.

items.docs: Use the provided `context`, `environments`, and `projects` to evaluate toggles on this Unleash instance. You can use comma-separated values to provide multiple values to each context field. Returns a combinatorial list of all toggles that match the parameters and what they evaluate to. The response also contains the input parameters that were provided.

info.name: Export Feature Flags From an Environment

items.docs: Exports all features listed in the `features` property from the environment specified in the request body. If set to `true`, the `downloadFile` property will let you download a file with the exported data. Otherwise, the export data is returned directly as JSON. Refer to the documentation for more information about [Unleash's export functionality](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/import-export#export-feature-flags).

Validates a feature flag data set. Checks whether the data can be imported into the specified project and environment. The returned value is an object that contains errors, warnings, and permissions required to perform the import, as described in the [import documentation](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/import-export#import-feature-flags).
[Import feature flags](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/import-export#import-feature-flags) into a specific project and environment.

Provides statistics about various features of Unleash to allow for reporting of usage for self-hosted customers. The response contains data such as the number of users, groups, features, strategies, versions, etc.

This operation returns information about whether this Unleash instance is ready to serve requests or not. Typically used by your deployment orchestrator (e.g. Kubernetes, Docker Swarm, Mesos, et al.).

This operation returns information about whether this Unleash instance is healthy and ready to serve requests or not. Typically used by your deployment orchestrator (e.g. Kubernetes, Docker Swarm, Mesos, et al.).

info.name: Add a User Via a Signup Token

items.docs: Create a user with the viewer root role and link them to the provided signup token
Retrieves all existing public signup tokens.
Lets administrators create a invite link to share with colleagues.  People that join using the public invite are assigned the `Viewer` role
Get information about a specific token. The `:token` part of the URL should be the token's secret.
Update information about a specific token. The `:token` part of the URL should be the token's secret.
Retrieves all feature types that exist in this Unleash instance, along with their descriptions and lifetimes.

Updates the lifetime configuration for the specified [feature flag type](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/feature-flags#feature-flag-types). The expected lifetime is an integer representing the number of days before Unleash marks a feature flag of that type as potentially stale. If set to `null` or `0`, then feature flags of that particular type will never be marked as potentially stale.

When a feature flag type's expected lifetime is changed, this will also cause any feature flags of this t
This endpoint archives the specified feature.
This endpoint revives the specified feature from archive.
This endpoint deletes the specified features, that are in archive.
This endpoint revives the specified features.

Retrieves all strategy types ([predefined](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/activation-strategies "predefined strategies") and [custom strategies](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/activation-strategies#custom-strategies)) that are defined on this Unleash instance.
Creates a custom strategy type based on the supplied data.
Retrieves the definition of the strategy specified in the URL
Updates the specified strategy type. Any properties not specified in the request body are left untouched.
Deletes the specified strategy definition
Marks the specified strategy as deprecated.
Marks the specified strategy as not deprecated. If the strategy wasn't already deprecated, nothing changes.

info.name: Get Strategies That Use a Context Field

items.docs: Retrieves a list of all strategies that use the specified context field. If the context field doesn't exist, returns an empty list of strategies

info.name: Get Strategies That Use a Context Field

items.docs:
This is a beta endpoint and it may change or be removed in the future.

Retrieves a list of all strategies that use the specified context field. If the context field doesn't exist, returns an empty list of strategies

Sets the segments of the strategy specified to be exactly the ones passed in the payload. Any segments that were used by the strategy before will be removed if they are not in the provided list of segments.

info.name: Get the Most Recent Events From the Unleash Instance or All Events Related to a Project.

params.name: project
params.description: The name of the project whose events you want to retrieve

items.docs:
Returns **the last 100** events from the Unleash instance when called without a query parameter. When called with a `project` parameter, returns **all events** for the specified project.

If the provided project does not exist, the list of events will be empty.

info.name: Get All Events Related to a Specific Feature Flag.

items.docs: Returns all events related to the specified feature flag. If the feature flag does not exist, the list of events will be empty.

info.name: Get a List of All Users That Have Created Events

items.docs: Returns a list of all users that have created events in the system.

params.name: query
params.description: Find events by a free-text search query. The query will be matched against the event data payload (if any).

params.name: id
params.description: Filter by event ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

params.name: groupId
params.description: Filter by group ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

params.name: feature
params.description: Filter by feature name using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF

params.name: project
params.description: Filter by projects ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by event type using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

params.name: createdBy
params.description: Filter by the ID of the event creator, using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

params.name: from
params.description: The starting date of the creation date range in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: to
params.description: The ending date of the creation date range in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of features to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of feature environments to return in a page. By default it is set to 50. The maximum is 1000.

params.name: environment
params.description: Filter by environment name using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

items.docs: Allows searching for events that match the query parameter criteria.
Returns a list of unknown flag reports from the last 24 hours, if any. Maximum of 1000.

info.name: Get All Personal Access Tokens (pats) for the Current User.

items.docs: Returns all of the [personal access tokens](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#personal-access-tokens) (PATs) belonging to the current user.

info.name: Create a New Personal Access Token (pat) for the Current User.

items.docs: Creates a new [personal access token](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#personal-access-tokens (PAT) belonging to the current user.

info.name: Delete a Personal Access Token (pat) for the Current User.

params.name: id
params.description: a personal access token id

items.docs: Deletes a [personal access token](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#personal-access-tokens) (PAT) belonging to the current user.

params.name: include
params.description: Whether the response should include project-specific or root context fields in addition to the fields in the default response. When querying the root context API, `include=project` will yield a response that includes all project-specific context fields in addition to all root context fields. Conversely, when querying a project-specific context API, using `include=root` will yield a response that includes all root context fields in addition to the project-specific context fields. The other combinations have no effect, because the responses already include those fields. When including project-specific context fields via the root-level API, context fields in private projects the user does not have access to will be omitted.

items.docs: Returns all configured [Context fields](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/unleash-context) that have been created.
Endpoint that allows creation of [custom context fields](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/unleash-context#custom-context-fields)
Returns specific [context field](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/unleash-context) identified by the name in the path
Endpoint that allows updating a custom context field. Used to toggle stickiness and add/remove legal values for this context field

Endpoint that allows deletion of a custom context field. Does not validate that context field is not in use, but since context field configuration is stored in a json blob for the strategy, existing strategies are safe.

info.name: Add or Update Legal Value for the Context Field

items.docs: Endpoint that allows adding or updating a single custom context field legal value. If the legal value already exists, it will be updated with the new description

info.name: Delete Legal Value for the Context Field

items.docs: Removes the specified custom context field legal value. Does not validate that the legal value is not in use and does not remove usage from constraints that use it.
Check whether the provided data can be used to create a context field. If the data is not valid, returns a 400 status code with the reason why it is not valid.

params.name: include
params.description: Whether the response should include project-specific or root context fields in addition to the fields in the default response. When querying the root context API, `include=project` will yield a response that includes all project-specific context fields in addition to all root context fields. Conversely, when querying a project-specific context API, using `include=root` will yield a response that includes all root context fields in addition to the project-specific context fields. The other combinations have no effect, because the responses already include those fields. When including project-specific context fields via the root-level API, context fields in private projects the user does not have access to will be omitted.

List all tags available in Unleash.

info.name: List All Tags of a Given Type.

items.docs: List all tags of a given type. If the tag type does not exist it returns an empty list.

info.name: Get a Tag by Type and Value.

items.docs: Get a tag by type and value. Can be used to check whether a given tag already exists in Unleash or not.
Delete a tag by type and value. When a tag is deleted all references to the tag are removed.
Get a list of all available tag types.
Create a new tag type.

Validates whether if the body of the request is a valid tag and whether the a tag type with that name already exists or not. If a tag type with the same name exists, this operation will return a 409 status code.
Get a tag type by name.
Update the configuration for the specified tag type.
Deletes a tag type. If any features have tags of this type, those tags will be deleted.

info.name: Adds a Tag to the Specified Features

items.docs: Add a tag to a list of features. Create tags if needed.
Retrieve all addons and providers that are defined on this Unleash instance.
Create an addon instance. The addon must use one of the providers available on this Unleash instance.
Retrieve information about the addon whose ID matches the ID in the request URL.

Update the addon with a specific ID. Any fields in the update object will be updated. Properties that are not included in the update object will not be affected. To empty a property, pass `null` as that property's value.

Note: passing `null` as a value for the description property will set it to an empty string.
Delete the addon specified by the ID in the request path.

info.name: Get Integration Events for a Specific Integration Configuration.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of results to return in a page. By default it is set to 50.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of results to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

items.docs: Returns a list of integration events belonging to a specific integration configuration, identified by its id.
Retrieves all API tokens that exist in the Unleash instance.
Create an API token of a specific type: one of client, admin, frontend, backend.

Retrieves all API tokens that match a given token name. Because token names are not unique, this endpoint will always return a list. If no tokens with the provided name exist, the list will be empty. Otherwise, it will contain all the tokens with the given name.
Updates an existing API token with a new expiry date. The `token` path parameter is the token's `secret`. If the token does not exist, this endpoint returns a 200 OK, but does nothing.
Deletes an existing API token. The `token` path parameter is the token's `secret`. If the token does not exist, this endpoint returns a 200 OK, but does nothing.
Add a dependency to a parent feature. Each environment will resolve corresponding dependency independently.
Remove dependencies to all parent features.
Remove a dependency to a parent feature.
List available parents who have no transitive dependencies.
List available parent variants across all strategy variants and feature environment variants.
Check if any dependencies exist in this Unleash instance

items.docs: Uses the name provided in the body of the request to validate if the given name exists or not

params.name: id
params.description: a segment id

items.docs: Retrieve all strategies that reference the specified segment.

params.name: id
params.description: a segment id

items.docs: Retrieves a segment based on its ID.

params.name: id
params.description: a segment id

items.docs: Updates the content of the segment with the provided payload. Requires `name` and `constraints` to be present. If `project` is not present, it will be set to `null`. Any other fields not specified will be left untouched.

params.name: id
params.description: a segment id

items.docs: Deletes a segment by its id, if not found returns a 409 error
Retrieves all segments that exist in this Unleash instance.
Creates a new segment using the payload provided
Tells you whether maintenance mode is enabled or disabled
Lets administrators put Unleash into a mostly read-only mode. While Unleash is in maintenance mode, users can not change any configuration settings
Provides the configured settings for [telemetry information collection](https://docs.getunleash.io/topics/data-collection)

params.name: query
params.description: The search query for the feature name or tag

params.name: project
params.description: Id of the project where search and filter is performed. The project id can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

params.name: state
params.description: The state of the feature active/stale. The state can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

params.name: lifecycle
params.description: The lifecycle stage of the feature. The stagee can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

params.name: type
params.description: The feature flag type to filter by. The type can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

params.name: createdBy
params.description: The feature flag creator to filter by. The creators can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

params.name: tag
params.description: The list of feature tags to filter by. Feature tag has to specify a type and a value joined with a colon.

params.name: segment
params.description: The list of segments with operators to filter by. The segment valid operators are INCLUDE, DO_NOT_INCLUDE, INCLUDE_ALL_OF, INCLUDE_ANY_OF, EXCLUDE_IF_ANY_OF, EXCLUDE_ALL.

params.name: status
params.description: The list of feature environment status to filter by. Feature environment has to specify a name and a status joined with a colon.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of features to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of feature environments to return in a page. By default it is set to 50.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the results by. By default it is set to "createdAt".

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order for the sortBy. By default it is det to "asc".

params.name: favoritesFirst
params.description: The flag to indicate if the favorite features should be returned first. By default it is set to false.

params.name: archived
params.description: Whether to get results for archived feature flags or active feature flags. If `IS:true`, Unleash will return only archived flags. If `IS:false`, it will return only active flags.

params.name: createdAt
params.description: The date the feature was created. The date can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS_BEFORE, IS_ON_OR_AFTER.

params.name: lastSeenAt
params.description: The date the feature was last seen from metrics. The date can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS_BEFORE, IS_ON_OR_AFTER.

items.docs: Search and filter by selected fields.
